popularStory.headline: Charles Ier de Bourbon
popularStory.description:
Charles Ier de Bourbon, né en 1401 et mort le 4 décembre 1456 à Moulins, est duc de Bourbon et d'Auvergne de 1434 à 1456.

Biographie

Né en 1401, Charles Ier de Bourbon est le fils de Jean Ier, duc d …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Charles de Bourbon I於1401年10月27日出生在法國i萊-德-福朗斯芭黎絲時，他的父親Jean de Bourbon I - Troisième Maison de Bourbon20歲，母親Marie de Berry Duchess of Auvergne26歲。他於1425年9月17日與Anne de Bourgogne在法國布格-福朗-可特艾薩o-吉洛易奧圖結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1456年12月4日在法國奧維阿利也姆林斯享年55歲，埋葬在法國奧維-o-阿爾培斯阿利也索維尼。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Charles de Bourbon I于1401年10月27日出生在法国i萊-德-福朗斯芭黎絲时，他的父亲Jean de Bourbon I - Troisième Maison de Bourbon20岁，母亲Marie de Berry Duchess of Auvergne26岁。他于1425年9月17日与Anne de Bourgogne在法国布格-福朗-可特艾薩o-吉洛易奧圖结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1456年12月4日在法国奧維阿利也姆林斯享年55岁，埋葬在法国奧維-o-阿爾培斯阿利也索維尼。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bourbon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: habitational name from a village in Allier, the site of the (now ruined) castle of Bourbon, or from another place called (Le) Bourbon, mainly in the southern part of France. The placename is derived from a Celtic and pre-Celtic element borb- denoting a hot spring.

History: The progenitor of a large part of the American bearers of the surname Bourbon was Jean-Pierre Bourbon from Salins-les-Bains in Jura, France, who settled in MO in the middle of the 19th century. However, many bearers of the surname claim a connection with the house of Bourbon, which provided generations of monarchs of France and Spain and other European royalty. It takes its name from the castle of Bourbon in Allier, which was held by Adhémar, a 9th-century noble. His descendant Beatrice, heiress of Bourbon, married Robert of Clermont, 6th son of King Louis IX of France, in 1272, and these two are considered founders of the royal house. Robert's son Louis was created Duke of Bourbon in 1327. By the 16th century they had added much of southern France, the dukedom of Vendôme, and the kingdom of Navarre to their fiefs, and in 1589 a Bourbon succeeded to the throne of France as Henry IV. His grandson, Louis XIV, called ‘the Sun King’, reigned 1643–1715, presiding over a golden age of French literature and art and attempting to establish French supremacy in Europe. The Bourbons ruled France until the Revolution in 1793 and again 1815–48. The present claimants to the French throne are descended from Louis XIV's brother, Philippe, Duke of Orléans. The Bourbons acquired the Spanish throne in 1700, when a grandson of Louis XIV succeeded as Philip V; they ruled until 1931 and were restored, in the person of King Juan Carlos, in 1975. The Bourbon kings of the Two Sicilies (i.e. Sicily and Naples), 1759–1861, and Bourbon dukes of Parma, 1748–1860, were descended from the Spanish branch of the family.

variants.name: Borbon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Ana, Jesus, Marta, Miguel Angel, Tomas, Ademar, Alfredo, Ana Maria, Bernabe, Berta, Carlos.

nameMeanings.name: Charles
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Germanic word, karl, meaning ‘free man’, akin to Old English ceorl ‘man’. The name, Latin form Carolus, owed its popularity in medieval Europe to the Frankish leader Charlemagne ( ?742–814 ), who in 800 established himself as Holy Roman Emperor. His name (Latin Carolus Magnus) means ‘Charles the Great’. Carolus—or Karl, the German form—was a common name among Frankish leaders, including Charlemagne's grandfather Charles Martel ( 688–741 ). Charles is the French form. The name occurs occasionally in medieval Britain as Karolus or Carolus; it had a certain vogue in West Yorkshire from the 1400s, particularly among gentry families. The form Charles was chosen by Mary Queen of Scots ( 1542–87 ), who had been brought up in France, for her son, Charles James ( 1566–1625 ), who became King James VI of Scotland and, from 1603 , James I of England. His son and grandson both reigned as King Charles , and the name thus became established in the 17th century both in the Stuart royal house and among English and Scottish supporters of the Stuart monarchy. In the 18th century it was to some extent favoured, along with James , by Jacobites, supporters of the exiled Stuarts, opposed to the Hanoverian monarchy, especially in the Highlands of Scotland. In the 19th century the popularity of the name was further enhanced by romanticization of the story of ‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’, leader of the 1745 rebellion.

en.lifeSummary: When Charles de Bourbon I was born on 27 October 1401, in Paris, Île-de-France, France, his father, Jean de Bourbon I - Troisième Maison de Bourbon, was 20 and his mother, Marie de Berry Duchess of Auvergne, was 26. He married Anne de Bourgogne on 17 September 1425, in Autun, Saône-et-Loire, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, France. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 4 December 1456, in Moulins, Allier, Auvergne, France, at the age of 55, and was buried in Souvigny, Allier, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Charles de Bourbon I est né le 27 octobre 1401, à Paris, Île-de-France, France, son père, Jean de Bourbon I - Troisième Maison de Bourbon, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Marie de Berry Duchess of Auvergne, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Anne de Bourgogne le 17 septembre 1425, à Autun, Saône-et-Loire, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, France. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 4 décembre 1456, à Moulins, Allier, Auvergne, France, à l’âge de 55 ans, et a été enterré à Souvigny, Allier, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Charles de Bourbon I am 27. Oktober 1401 in Paris, Île-de-France, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Jean de Bourbon I - Troisième Maison de Bourbon 20 und seine Mutter Marie de Berry Duchess of Auvergne 26. Er hat am 17. September 1425 in Autun, Saône-et-Loire, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Frankreich Anne de Bourgogne geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 4. Dezember 1456 im Alter von 55 Jahren in Moulins, Allier, Auvergne, Frankreich und wurde in Souvigny, Allier, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Charles de Bourbon I è nato il il 27 ottobre 1401 nel Paris, Île-de-France, Francia, il suo padre, Jean de Bourbon I - Troisième Maison de Bourbon, aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, Marie de Berry Duchess of Auvergne, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Anne de Bourgogne il 17 settembre 1425, nel Autun, Saône-et-Loire, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 4 dicembre 1456, nel Moulins, Allier, Auvergne, Francia, all'età di 55 anni, e fu sepolto a Souvigny, Allier, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Charles de Bourbon Iが1401年10月27日にフランスiレ-デ-フランスパーイスで生まれた時，父Jean de Bourbon I - Troisième Maison de Bourbonは20歳，母Marie de Berry Duchess of Auvergneは26歳であった。1425年9月17日にフランスボウルゴグネ-フランチェ-コムトeサoネ-エト-ロイアーアウトゥンでAnne de Bourgogneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1456年12月4日にフランスアウバーグンアリールモウリンスで55歳で死去し，フランスアウバーグネ-ルhoネ-アルペスアリールソウヴィグニーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Charles de Bourbon I(이)가 1401년 10월 27일 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 파리스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Jean de Bourbon I - Troisième Maison de Bourbon(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Marie de Berry Duchess of Auvergne(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1425년 9월 17일 프랑스 부르고그니-프랭체-코트e 사o니-트-로이르 오툰에서 Anne de Bourgogne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1456년 12월 4일 프랑스 오버그니 앨리어 모우린스에서 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 오버그니-ho니-알프스 앨리어 소우비그니에 묻혔다.

mn.27 October 1401–4 December 1456: 1401 оны Аравдугаар сарын 27–1456 оны Арванхоёрдугаар сарын 4

pt.27 October 1401–4 December 1456: 27 de outubro de 1401–4 de dezembro de 1456
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Charles de Bourbon I nasceu em 27 de outubro de 1401, em Paris, Île-de-France, França, seu pai, Jean de Bourbon I - Troisième Maison de Bourbon, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Marie de Berry Duchess of Auvergne, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Anne de Bourgogne em 17 de setembro de 1425, em Autun, Saône-et-Loire, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 4 de dezembro de 1456, em Moulins, Allier, Auvergne, França, com 55 anos, e foi sepultado em Souvigny, Allier, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Charles de Bourbon I родился 27 октября 1401 г. в местности пэрис, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция, его отцу по имени Jean de Bourbon I - Troisième Maison de Bourbon было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Marie de Berry Duchess of Auvergne, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Anne de Bourgogne 17 сентября 1425 г. в местности аучунь, саoне-ет-лоир, бургогне-фрэнче-комтe, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер 4 декабря 1456 г. в местности моулинс, аллиир, аувиргн, Франция в возрасте 55 лет и был похоронен в местности соувигни, аллиир, аувиргне-рoне-алпесь, Франция.

sm.Bet. 1434–1456: mai le 1434 e o'o I le 1456

es.27 October 1401–4 December 1456: 27 de octubre de 1401–4 de diciembre de 1456
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Charles de Bourbon I nació el 27 de octubre de 1401, en Paris, Île-de-France, Francia, su papá, Jean de Bourbon I - Troisième Maison de Bourbon, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Marie de Berry Duchess of Auvergne, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Anne de Bourgogne el 17 de septiembre de 1425, en Autun, Saône-et-Loire, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 4 de diciembre de 1456, en Moulins, Allier, Auvergne, Francia, a la edad de 55 años, y fue sepultado en Souvigny, Allier, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Francia.
